payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Task claiming process enabling children to register completed work
payload.justification: Claiming completed tasks represents a core strategic process central to the enterprise purpose.

payload.description: Reward definition process for assigning monetary values to tasks
payload.justification: Defining task-based rewards is a strategic process performed by parental stakeholders to establish the compensation model.

payload.description: Child compensation process executed by parental role
payload.justification: Compensating children appropriately is identified as a key strategic process requiring parental oversight.

payload.description: Visibility provision function for task opportunities and completed work
payload.justification: Providing visibility represents a strategic function supporting both opportunity awareness and completion tracking.

payload.description: Earnings transparency function for weekly financial reporting
payload.justification: Weekly earnings transparency is a strategic function ensuring stakeholder awareness of financial outcomes.

payload.description: Oversight reporting function supporting parental governance
payload.justification: Oversight reporting is explicitly identified as a strategic function enabling parental role fulfillment.

payload.description: Historical record-keeping function for audit and review
payload.justification: Record-keeping capability represents a strategic function requirement, though expressed as a system goal.

payload.description: Task available for completion by children
payload.justification: Tasks are the core strategic entity representing work units that children complete for monetary rewards, explicitly mentioned in SG916 and SG917.

payload.description: Pocket money earned by children
payload.justification: Pocket money is the fundamental monetary entity resulting from task completion, central to the strategic purpose across multiple signals.

payload.description: Historical record of task completion and earnings
payload.justification: Historical records represent a strategic data entity required for system capability, though expressed as a requirement in SG921.

payload.description: Task completion claim relationship between children and tasks
payload.justification: The claiming action establishes a strategic relationship between child stakeholders and task entities as the primary interaction model.

payload.description: Monetary reward assignment relationship between tasks and pocket money
payload.justification: Task-based monetary rewards define a fundamental strategic relationship linking work to compensation.

payload.description: Weekly earnings cycle for pocket money accumulation
payload.justification: The weekly timeframe for earnings and transparency represents a strategic temporal cycle governing the enterprise.

payload.description: Child stakeholder claiming tasks and earning pocket money
payload.justification: Children are clearly identified as primary actors performing task claims and receiving earnings transparency.

payload.description: Parental role defining rewards and providing compensation
payload.justification: Parents fulfill a strategic role in establishing monetary rewards and compensating children, requiring oversight capability.

payload.description: Enable task-based pocket money earning for children
payload.justification: The core strategic goal is explicitly stated as enabling children to claim completed tasks for pocket money.

payload.description: Establish task-based monetary reward system
payload.justification: Parental motivation to define task-based rewards represents a strategic objective for the enterprise.

payload.description: Provide child visibility into available task opportunities
payload.justification: Visibility into available opportunities is articulated as a distinct strategic goal for child stakeholders.

payload.description: Provide child visibility into completed work
payload.justification: Visibility into completed work represents a separate strategic goal supporting stakeholder awareness.

payload.description: Achieve weekly earnings transparency for children
payload.justification: Weekly earnings transparency is established as a strategic objective for child stakeholder engagement.

payload.description: Enable parental oversight of child compensation
payload.justification: Parental oversight capability is defined as a strategic need and system objective.

payload.description: Maintain historical records for accountability and review
payload.justification: Historical record-keeping is explicitly stated as a strategic system goal.

payload.description: Appropriate compensation principle guiding parental role execution
payload.justification: The concept of 'appropriately' compensating children implies a strategic principle governing fair reward distribution.

payload.description: Task creation function enabling parent to define new task entities with monetary values
payload.justification: SG942 defines parent capability to create task entities as core business function

payload.description: Task visibility function providing child access to view available and completed tasks
payload.justification: SG944 defines child visibility requirements as business function for task access

payload.description: Earnings aggregation function calculating weekly totals for child entities
payload.justification: SG945 establishes weekly aggregation as business function showing total earnings

payload.description: Parent reporting function aggregating child earnings and completed tasks for compensation determination
payload.justification: SG946 defines parent-facing reporting capability as business function for compensation purposes

payload.description: Weekly task reset rule returning completed tasks to available state
payload.justification: SG947 explicitly specifies weekly reset as business rule governing task state transitions

payload.description: Historical data retention rule mandating persistence of completed task records
payload.justification: SG948 mandates retention of historical task data as business rule for record-keeping

payload.description: Task entity representing units of work assignable to children
payload.justification: SG941 establishes task-based system core concept; SG942 explicitly defines task entities with monetary attributes

payload.description: Child entity representing user who completes tasks and earns compensation
payload.justification: SG941 establishes children as core business concept; SG943 and SG944 reference child entities in relationships and visibility rules

payload.description: Parent entity responsible for task creation and compensation administration
payload.justification: SG942 defines parent capability to create tasks; SG946 establishes parent-facing reporting for compensation

payload.description: Monetary value attribute associated with task entity
payload.justification: SG942 explicitly defines monetary value as attribute of task entities

payload.description: Task state attribute indicating available or completed status
payload.justification: SG944 references available and completed states; SG947 defines reset returning tasks to available state

payload.description: Weekly earnings total attribute aggregated for child entity
payload.justification: SG945 establishes weekly aggregation showing total earnings as derived attribute

payload.description: Task completion relationship linking child entity to task entity
payload.justification: SG943 explicitly specifies relationship between task completion event and child entity

payload.description: Historical task data entity retained as persistent business record
payload.justification: SG948 mandates retention of historical task data as persistent business entity

payload.description: Task completion event marking child fulfillment of assigned work
payload.justification: SG943 explicitly references task completion event in relationship specification

payload.description: Weekly cycle defining temporal scope for task availability and earnings aggregation
payload.justification: SG945 and SG947 establish weekly cycle as business timing constraint for aggregation and reset

payload.description: Weekly reset trigger initiating task state transition to available status
payload.justification: SG947 defines weekly reset as business trigger causing task state changes

payload.description: Parent role responsible for task creation and compensation administration
payload.justification: SG942 and SG946 define parent capabilities establishing this as distinct business role

payload.description: Child role responsible for viewing and completing tasks to earn compensation
payload.justification: SG943, SG944, and SG945 define child capabilities establishing this as distinct business role

payload.description: Track and compensate children for task completion through structured pocket money system
payload.justification: SG941 establishes core business goal of task-based pocket money tracking for children

payload.description: Child visibility constraint limited to temporal scope of current and completed tasks
payload.justification: SG944 defines visibility requirements as business constraint on information access

payload.description: Historical data preservation principle ensuring auditability of task completion records
payload.justification: SG948 mandates retention of historical data reflecting business principle of record preservation

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Child Participation
payload.description: Enterprise concern defining who may participate in task-based earning arrangements, their entitlements to engage with work opportunities, and the fundamental relationship between children and the household economic learning system.

payload.name: Parental Governance
payload.description: Enterprise concern establishing parental authority and accountability for defining economic terms, administering compensation, maintaining oversight of child earnings, and ensuring appropriate reward structures align with household values.

payload.name: Work Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise concern defining what constitutes recognized work within the household, how task opportunities are made available, the claiming process by which work is registered as completed, and the commitment to maintain a coherent inventory of work that children may undertake.

payload.name: Compensation Obligation
payload.description: Enterprise concern establishing the household's commitment to translate completed work into monetary rewards, the relationship between tasks and earnings, periodic settlement cycles, and the fundamental obligation to deliver pocket money earned by children.

payload.name: Record Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise concern for maintaining authoritative historical evidence of task completion and earnings transactions, enabling retrospective review, supporting audit requirements, and preserving the institutional memory necessary for accountability across all participants.

payload.name: Earnings Transparency
payload.description: Enterprise concern ensuring children have visibility into their financial position, completed contributions, and periodic earnings accumulation, supporting informed participation and trust in the household economic learning system.

payload.name: Work Assignment Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, publishing, and governing discrete units of compensable work within the household, including establishment of task definitions, associated value propositions, and lifecycle boundaries for assignment availability.

payload.name: Participation Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for child engagement with available work opportunities, encompassing visibility entitlements, fulfillment obligations, and the formal relationship between participant and assigned work that establishes compensable completion.

payload.name: Compensation Settlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for determining, aggregating, and reporting earnings entitlements arising from completed work, operating within defined temporal cycles to establish periodic compensation obligations owed to participating children.

payload.name: Performance Record Governance
payload.description: Business responsibility for maintaining persistent, auditable evidence of work completion events and resulting compensation obligations, ensuring historical integrity and accountability across the lifecycle of the pocket money system.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants in household economic learning arrangements.
payload.rationale: Establishes the fundamental membership relationship between children and the enterprise scope of household economic learning; derived from the Actor CCI identifying children as stakeholders.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to earn pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: Expresses the core enterprise commitment to provide a task-based earning capability; combines the goal of enabling task-based earning with the relationship mechanism (task completion claims) that implements it.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into available task opportunities.
payload.rationale: Distinct enterprise obligation to ensure children can see work opportunities; separate from the earning capability itself.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish and maintain authority for defining economic terms and reward structures within the household.
payload.rationale: Parental governance authority is the foundational constraint from which reward definition and compensation processes derive their legitimacy. The parental role defines rewards (Who-002), the reward definition process assigns monetary values (How-002), and the appropriate compensation principle guides execution (Why-008) — collectively establishing enterprise-level authority.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide compensation to children for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: The child compensation process (How-003) executed by the parental role (Who-002) constitutes the enterprise's functional obligation to deliver compensation. This is a capability the enterprise commits to provide.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain oversight of child earnings and compensation activities.
payload.rationale: The oversight reporting function (How-006) directly supports the goal of enabling parental oversight (Why-006). This is an enterprise-level commitment to govern and monitor compensation activities rather than a system capability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise tasks available for completion by children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise-level recognition of task as a structural entity within the household operating scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a task claiming process enabling children to register completed work.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to providing a defined process by which children may claim and register work completion.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into task opportunities.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to making task opportunities visible; sourced from both the visibility provision function and the goal of providing child visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into completed work.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to making completed work visible to children; derived from the visibility provision function and the explicit goal statement.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a task-based monetary reward system.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to govern task completion through a monetary reward mechanism; sourced from Why-column goal content.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide monetary rewards to children for completed work.
payload.rationale: Core enterprise obligation to translate completed tasks into pocket money, derived from the entity (pocket money earned), the process (child compensation), and the foundational commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a relationship between tasks and monetary reward values.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion defining the association between work units (tasks) and compensation units (pocket money amounts) at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to earn pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment derived from the Why-column goal to enable task-based earning capability; expresses a governance rule governing the earning mechanism.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall settle pocket money earnings on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Temporal commitment establishing the periodicity of compensation settlement; derived from the weekly earnings cycle CCI.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings are accumulated and settled at seven-day intervals.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure transparency of weekly earnings to children.
payload.rationale: Governance constraint derived from the Why-column goal requiring visibility and clarity of compensation amounts over the weekly cycle.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records of task completion and earnings transactions.
payload.rationale: All three CCIs converge on a single enterprise obligation: the preservation of authoritative historical evidence. CCI-What-003 identifies the entity (historical record), CCI-How-007 describes the record-keeping function, and CCI-Why-007 expresses the motivating goal. Together they define one functional obligation at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable retrospective review and audit of task completion and earnings transactions.
payload.rationale: The record-keeping function (CCI-How-007) explicitly supports audit and review purposes, and the goal (CCI-Why-007) emphasizes accountability and review. This is a distinct capability obligation from record maintenance: the enterprise must not only maintain records but also enable their use for audit and retrospective analysis.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into their completed contributions and financial position.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to transparency, derived from the Why-column goal of providing child visibility into completed work. This is a governance commitment rather than a function.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure weekly earnings transparency for children.
payload.rationale: Enterprise-level commitment to transparency on a weekly cycle, combining the Why-column goal with the temporal When-column constraint. This expresses an enterprise governance obligation rather than a system function.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings information is made accessible to children on a weekly basis.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish weekly financial reporting of earnings accumulation.
payload.rationale: Enterprise capability to report financial information on a weekly cycle, derived from the How-column function combined with the When-column cycle. This is an enterprise-level capability commitment.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each discrete unit of work assignable to participants.
payload.rationale: Task entity requires the business to maintain structured records of assignable work units at the business responsibility level.

payload.statement: The Parent shall be responsible for defining new work assignments.
payload.rationale: Parent role accountable for task creation function translates to business-level authority and responsibility.

payload.statement: The Parent shall govern the compensation associated with each work assignment.
payload.rationale: Parent entity's compensation administration responsibility is a core business stewardship capability.

payload.statement: Each work assignment shall have an associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: Monetary value attribute defines a structural composition requirement for the task entity at business level.

payload.statement: Each work assignment shall have a state indicating its availability or completion status.
payload.rationale: Task state attribute defines a structural attribute requirement necessary for business lifecycle governance.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly cycle governing work assignment availability and earnings aggregation.
payload.rationale: Weekly cycle defines a temporal governance rule constraining business operations at the business-owner level.

payload.statement: The business shall restore completed work assignments to available status on a weekly basis.
payload.rationale: Weekly task reset rule and trigger define a recurring business capability to steward task lifecycle, not a workflow sequence.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise the child as the business role accountable for completing assigned work and earning compensation.
payload.rationale: The child entity and child role together define the business relationship establishing who may participate in compensable work. This is a structural declaration of role membership and accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child's completion of assigned work.
payload.rationale: The task completion relationship and completion event together establish the business obligation to record fulfillment of work assignments as the basis for compensation.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to each child visibility of work opportunities within the temporal scope of current and completed assignments.
payload.rationale: The task visibility function combined with the temporal visibility constraint defines the business capability to provide scoped access to work information. The constraint governs the extent of visibility, making both elements necessary to express the complete business responsibility.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child's weekly earnings total.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column attribute defining the business artefact that must be maintained to support compensation settlement.

payload.statement: The business shall determine weekly earnings totals for each child participant.
payload.rationale: HOW-column aggregation capability operating within WHEN-column weekly temporal scope — the business responsibility to calculate compensation entitlements.

payload.statement: The parent role shall be accountable for compensation administration.
payload.rationale: WHO-column role accountability for the settlement domain — establishes business responsibility at the parent-actor level.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to the parent role aggregated earnings and completed task information for each child.
payload.rationale: HOW-column reporting capability enabling the WHO-column parent role to perform compensation determination responsibilities.

payload.statement: The business shall account for child compensation on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: WHEN-column temporal cycle governing the periodicity of the business settlement obligation.

payload.statement: The business shall compensate children for completed tasks through a structured pocket money arrangement.
payload.rationale: WHY-column goal defining the fundamental business responsibility — while motivation-sourced, it describes the capability the business must deliver rather than constraining behaviour.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a persistent record of each completed task.
payload.rationale: What-column entity (historical task data) and How-column rule (retention mandate) together establish the business responsibility to maintain completed task records; 'maintain' is business-level vocabulary for the persistent accountability obligation.

payload.statement: The business shall be accountable for the auditability of task completion records.
payload.rationale: Why-column principle (historical data preservation for auditability) imposes a governance constraint on how the business must steward completion evidence; this is a business rule governing record integrity, not a capability declaration.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed tasks for pocket money throughout the week.
Identifies the parental motivation to define task-based monetary rewards as a system objective.
Articulates the goal of providing children visibility into both available opportunities and their completed work.
Establishes the objective of weekly earnings transparency for child stakeholders.
Defines the parental need for oversight reporting to fulfill their role in compensating children appropriately.
States the requirement for historical record-keeping capability as a system goal.
Document section header indicating the beginning of conceptual system overview content.
Establishes the core business concept of a task-based pocket money tracking system for children.
Defines parent capability to create task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies relationship between task completion event and child entity.
Defines child visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation concept showing total earnings for child entities.
Defines parent-facing reporting capability aggregating child earnings and completed tasks for compensation purposes.
Specifies weekly reset business rule returning tasks to available state.
Mandates retention of historical task data as persistent business entity.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Stakeholder elements present in the ledger.
